params.containerId: string (DOM id of the element that will host the Leaflet map; element must already be in the DOM and have non-zero size)
params.events: Array<EarthquakeEvent> (normalized events as defined in section-1--section-2.json)

shape.markerCount: integer (number of circle markers actually placed on the map)

magnitudeColor.returns: string (hex/rgb color usable in Leaflet circleMarker fillColor; used by section-3 to render the legend swatches consistently with the markers)

magnitudeRadius.returns: number (pixel radius for the circle marker; used by section-3 to size the legend swatches consistently with the markers)

notes: section-2 exposes mount() plus the two pure styling functions magnitudeColor() and magnitudeRadius() so section-3 can build a legend whose swatches exactly match what's rendered on the map (single source of truth for encoding). Legend doesn't reach into Leaflet — it just calls the styling functions and renders ordinary HTML/CSS swatches.
